key_insights.insight: Failure is often a byproduct of societal expectations, not an objective measure of worth.
key_insights.quote: Failure is like a byproduct of social expectations, as is success.

key_insights.insight: External validation is a trap; true self-worth comes from within and from a few cornerstone relationships.
key_insights.quote: My only validation that means anything can come from within and from my cornerstone relationships.

key_insights.insight: Social media algorithms create unhealthy comparison bubbles, so use social media consciously by unfollowing toxic accounts and muting notifications.
key_insights.quote: Use social media in a much more conscious way...unfollow people that are bad for you. Turn off your notifications.

key_insights.insight: Sometimes it's important to stand up for yourself and calmly state your position, especially when the integrity of who you are and what you do is attacked.
key_insights.quote: Sometimes it is worth drawing breath and saying something calmly and just stating your position.

key_insights.insight: People pleasing, while stemming from a desire to be nice, becomes selfish because it prevents self-discovery and leads to inauthentic relationships.
key_insights.quote: People pleasing can start from a desire to be nice...but taken to its extreme...it actually becomes very selfish because you never take the time to know who you truly are.

key_insights.insight: In relationships, not letting go of the past prevents new beginnings.
key_insights.quote: Not letting it end is part...of the reason why something new doesn't start.

key_insights.insight: When we choose to share our vulnerabilities, we experience the most profound connections with others, and what we think of as our most personal shame turns out to have the most universal resonance.
key_insights.quote: When we choose to share our vulnerabilities is when we feel most satisfaction...most real connections with other people...what you think of as your most personal shame turns out to have most universal resonance.

key_insights.insight: It's important to have a 'safe space' and to know the right outlet for speaking about struggles or mental health issues.
key_insights.quote: The key thing is like knowing the right outlet for that...the safe space.

key_insights.insight: We are not our worst thoughts; thoughts are merely produced by the brain, and meditation is a way to observe and challenge negative thought patterns, train the brain to think differently, and replace negative thoughts with positive ones.
key_insights.quote: We are not our worst thoughts, that our thoughts are produced by our brain as organic matter... you can train your brain to think differently and to replace negative thoughts with positive ones.

actionable_advice.advice: Curate your social media feed to create a healthier context.
actionable_advice.how_to: Unfollow toxic accounts, mute notifications, and limit time spent on platforms that trigger comparison and self-doubt.

actionable_advice.advice: Challenge your thoughts by separating yourself from them.
actionable_advice.how_to: Practice meditation or mindfulness to observe your thoughts without judgment. Remind yourself that your thoughts are not necessarily reality.

actionable_advice.advice: Define your own metrics for success.
actionable_advice.how_to: Identify your values and passions, and set goals that align with them, rather than chasing external validation or societal expectations.

actionable_advice.advice: Identify your 'love language' and communicate your needs to your partner.
actionable_advice.how_to: Take a love language quiz to determine how you best receive and give love and discuss these needs with your partner.

actionable_advice.advice: Allow yourself to feel emotions after a heartbreak, setting a manageable time frame to feel miserable.
actionable_advice.how_to: Google 'how long does it take to get over heartbreak', commit to the acceptance of feeling it and understand that it somehow makes you feel better.

memorable_stories: Elizabeth shares the story of facing criticism for posting a socially distanced walk during lockdown and how she processed it.
Elizabeth shares Mo Gowdat's story about training his brain to reframe his grief after his son's death.
Elizabeth shares her experiences with infertility, miscarriage, and divorce and how those experiences led her to a place of vulnerability and authentic connection.
contrarian_claims: Rejection can be protection: Elizabeth stated her believe that "If you're rejected, that person is not for you."
Elizabeth challenges the notion that there is such a thing as 'too vulnerable': Elizabeth argues that there isn't a concept such as 'too vulnerable' but that there is a concept of oversharing.

Elizabeth said, that oversharing "attack[s] that kind of narrative because I feel that so many people feel shame and stigma over things they don't need to feel it for and it's because people stay silent."
episode_title: The Diary Of A CEO with Steven Bartlett - Elizabeth Day Opens Up About Heartbreak, Miscarriage & Failure
